tossups.question: In a painting titled for one of these things, a man in a green turban reaches over to grasp an upside-down man with no shirt. Six men cling to debris in the shape of a cross while the sun’s brightness forms an indistinct shape through clouds in that painting titled for a “ninth” one of these things by Ivan Aivazovsky. Along with birds, thin white brushstrokes upon a dark textured region near the base represent these things in a painting of a (*) monk in a landscape by Caspar David Friedrich. An artist made extensive use of the newly-available pigment Prussian blue to depict one of these things in the most famous print of a series that also depicts its subject in red on a “clear morning.” For 10 points, Mount Fuji is seen beneath what kind of thing in a print by Hokusai of one of these things “off Kanagawa”?
tossups.question_sanitized: In a painting titled for one of these things, a man in a green turban reaches over to grasp an upside-down man with no shirt. Six men cling to debris in the shape of a cross while the sun's brightness forms an indistinct shape through clouds in that painting titled for a "ninth" one of these things by Ivan Aivazovsky. Along with birds, thin white brushstrokes upon a dark textured region near the base represent these things in a painting of a (*) monk in a landscape by Caspar David Friedrich. An artist made extensive use of the newly-available pigment Prussian blue to depict one of these things in the most famous print of a series that also depicts its subject in red on a "clear morning." For 10 points, Mount Fuji is seen beneath what kind of thing in a print by Hokusai of one of these things "off Kanagawa"?
tossups.answer: a wave [accept The Ninth Wave or The Great Wave off Kanagawa]
tossups.answer_sanitized: a wave [accept The Ninth Wave or The Great Wave off Kanagawa]

tossups.question: A series titled for these locations features a painting in which multiple shirtless men drag baskets across red ground. The artist of that series created two artworks depicting “masculine” and “feminine” versions of a subject in these locations. A large black animal in this location appears in a work titled for being set “off Gōto” from a series of artworks titled for these locations “of Wisdom.” In another series, an artwork depicting one of these locations is followed by a depiction of a bright (*) red mountain titled Fine Wind, Clear Morning. Import of Prussian blue from the Netherlands revolutionized Japanese depictions of these general locations, including a print in which Mount Fuji can be seen in the background of a scene set “off Kanagawa.” For 10 points, in what general locations did Hokusai depict many fishers and whalers?
tossups.question_sanitized: A series titled for these locations features a painting in which multiple shirtless men drag baskets across red ground. The artist of that series created two artworks depicting "masculine" and "feminine" versions of a subject in these locations. A large black animal in this location appears in a work titled for being set "off Goto" from a series of artworks titled for these locations "of Wisdom." In another series, an artwork depicting one of these locations is followed by a depiction of a bright (*) red mountain titled Fine Wind, Clear Morning. Import of Prussian blue from the Netherlands revolutionized Japanese depictions of these general locations, including a print in which Mount Fuji can be seen in the background of a scene set "off Kanagawa." For 10 points, in what general locations did Hokusai depict many fishers and whalers?
tossups.answer: oceans [or seas or umi; accept One Thousand Pictures of the Ocean, One Thousand Pictures of the Sea, or Oceans of Wisdom; prompt on waves, Masculine Wave, Feminine Wave, or Great Wave off Kanagawa with “in what general location?”; prompt on bodies of water]
tossups.answer_sanitized: oceans [or seas or umi; accept One Thousand Pictures of the Ocean, One Thousand Pictures of the Sea, or Oceans of Wisdom; prompt on waves, Masculine Wave, Feminine Wave, or Great Wave off Kanagawa with "in what general location?"; prompt on bodies of water]

tossups.question: Paul Cézanne (“say-ZAN”) effusively praised the “depth of ages” evident in one of Gustave Courbet’s (“koor-BAY’s”) many late paintings of these subjects. Edmond Goncourt (“gon-KOOR”) praised the “animals’ claws” in a painting of one of these entities that he popularized in France. A sculpture titled for one of these subjects, which employs onyx marble and incorporates female figures from The Gossips, was created by Camille Claudel. A famous painting of one of these entities best exemplifies the (*) “blue revolution” enabled by its artist’s use of Prussian blue. The cover of Claude Debussy’s original score for La Mer (“mair”) was a print of one of these entities featuring several thin, fragile boats. For 10 points, one of Katsu·shika Hokusai’s Thirty-Six Views of Mount Fuji depicts what type of phenomenon “off Kanagawa”?
tossups.answer: waves [or the Great Wave off Kanagawa; accept tsunami; prompt on seas or oceans]
tossups.answer_sanitized: waves [or the Great Wave off Kanagawa; accept tsunami; prompt on seas or oceans]
tossups.question_sanitized: Paul Cezanne ("say-ZAN") effusively praised the "depth of ages" evident in one of Gustave Courbet's ("koor-BAY's") many late paintings of these subjects. Edmond Goncourt ("gon-KOOR") praised the "animals' claws" in a painting of one of these entities that he popularized in France. A sculpture titled for one of these subjects, which employs onyx marble and incorporates female figures from The Gossips, was created by Camille Claudel. A famous painting of one of these entities best exemplifies the (*) "blue revolution" enabled by its artist's use of Prussian blue. The cover of Claude Debussy's original score for La Mer ("mair") was a print of one of these entities featuring several thin, fragile boats. For 10 points, one of Katsushika Hokusai's Thirty-Six Views of Mount Fuji depicts what type of phenomenon "off Kanagawa"?

tossups.question: The Aboriginal artist Lin Onus depicted a dingo in a parody of this work titled Michael and I are just slipping down to the pub for a minute. Camille Claudel used onyx and bronze for a sculpture similar to this work depicting three nude women holding hands. A photograph of Igor Stravinsky in Claude Debussy’s apartment shows this work framed on the wall, which Debussy had earlier used as the cover for a set of “three symphonic sketches.” This was the first work in a series that extensively used (*) Prussian blue imported from the Netherlands. This work, which was followed in its series by Fine Wind, Clear Morning, depicts three long fishing barges caught in the title phenomenon. The snow-capped Mount Fuji can be seen in the background of, for 10 points, what Katsushika Hokusai woodblock print depicting the title marine phenomenon?
tossups.answer: The Great Wave off Kanagawa [or Under the Wave off Kanagawa]
tossups.answer_sanitized: The Great Wave off Kanagawa [or Under the Wave off Kanagawa]
tossups.question_sanitized: The Aboriginal artist Lin Onus depicted a dingo in a parody of this work titled Michael and I are just slipping down to the pub for a minute. Camille Claudel used onyx and bronze for a sculpture similar to this work depicting three nude women holding hands. A photograph of Igor Stravinsky in Claude Debussy's apartment shows this work framed on the wall, which Debussy had earlier used as the cover for a set of "three symphonic sketches." This was the first work in a series that extensively used (*) Prussian blue imported from the Netherlands. This work, which was followed in its series by Fine Wind, Clear Morning, depicts three long fishing barges caught in the title phenomenon. The snow-capped Mount Fuji can be seen in the background of, for 10 points, what Katsushika Hokusai woodblock print depicting the title marine phenomenon?

bonuses.leadin: An 1857 print copied by Vincent van Gogh shows a deep red sky obscured by one of these plants nicknamed the “Sleeping Dragon” at an estate in Kameido. For 10 points each:
parts: A pair of byōbu folding screens painted by Ogata Kōrin depict a swirling river bordered by the red and white blossoms of what plants?
Hiroshige painted plum orchards at Kameido and Kamada in two of his “One Hundred Famous Views” of this city, where the Rinpa school flourished.

Kōrin held two brushes in one hand to outline a Rinpa-style screen of these things, which surround Matsushima in a screen by Tawaraya Sōtatsu. The first of Hokusai’s “Thirty-six Views” depicts a “Great” one of these things in Prussian blue.
answers: plum trees [or ume; accept Red and White Plum Blossoms; prompt on trees]
Edo [accept One Hundred Famous Views of Edo; prompt on Tokyo]
waves [accept The Great Wave Off Kanagawa, Kanagawa-oki Nami Ura, Rough Waves, or Waves at Matsushima]

parts_sanitized: A pair of byobu folding screens painted by Ogata Korin depict a swirling river bordered by the red and white blossoms of what plants?
Hiroshige painted plum orchards at Kameido and Kamada in two of his "One Hundred Famous Views" of this city, where the Rinpa school flourished.

Korin held two brushes in one hand to outline a Rinpa-style screen of these things, which surround Matsushima in a screen by Tawaraya Sotatsu. The first of Hokusai's "Thirty-six Views" depicts a "Great" one of these things in Prussian blue.

bonuses.leadin: Copies of the Irises series Ogata Korin painted on these objects may have inspired Vincent van Gogh's own Irises. For 10 points each:
parts: Name these objects which diverged from similar fusama through the usage of tools like zenigata. The Kano School introduced gold leaf to paintings on these objects like one of cypress trees in Momoyama castle style.
The painter monk Sesson Shukei likely inspired Ogata's painting of these things, which are "rougher" than the "Great" one depicted in the first print of Hokusai's Thirty-Six Views of Mount Fuji.

Korin revived the Rinpa school with his brother Kenzan, a painter better known for art made in this material studied by Bernard Leach. Jomon artisans made "rope-patterned" art objects from this material using sancai colors during the Tang Dynasty.
answers: waves [or Rough Waves; or Great Wave off Kanagawa; or nami; or aranami; or Kanagawa-oki Nami Ura]
ceramics [or pottery; or clay; or earthenware; or porcelain; or brick; or obvious equivalents]

ceramics [or pottery; or clay; or earthenware; or porcelain; or brick; or obvious equivalents]

bonuses.leadin: Art from this country includes narrative handscrolls called E-maki. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this country, which is home to artists of many ukiyo-e woodcuts. Among the most famous series of artworks from this country is 36 Views of Mount Fuji.
This ukiyo-e work by Hokusai shows three boats being swept up by the title phenomenon.
In more modern times, this Japanese artist and coiner of the term “superflat” is famous for manga-inspired works like Flower Matango and Jellyfish Eyes.
answers_sanitized: Great Wave Off Kanagawa [or Kanagawa-Oki Nami-Ura]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Art from this country includes narrative handscrolls called E-maki. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this country, which is home to artists of many ukiyo-e woodcuts. Among the most famous series of artworks from this country is 36 Views of Mount Fuji.
This ukiyo-e work by Hokusai shows three boats being swept up by the title phenomenon.
In more modern times, this Japanese artist and coiner of the term "superflat" is famous for manga-inspired works like Flower Matango and Jellyfish Eyes.
